groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Topic, goal, audience, takeaway, facts, language, format, sender, CTA, length.

groups.title: Message & Structure
groups.description: Sources, sections, opening, links, timing, series context, exclusions.

groups.title: Brand & Delivery
groups.description: Tone, voice, platform, formatting, personalisation, sign-off, replies.

groups.title: Evidence & Compliance
groups.description: CTA URL, claims, disclosures, sensitive topics, and jurisdiction.

variables.label: Newsletter topic
variables.placeholder: What is this newsletter issue about?
variables.hint: Describe the main subject, update, story, offer, or theme.

help.why: The model needs a clear subject around which to build the entire issue.
help.example: Honest Early Access update and what shipped this week
help.avoid: Several unrelated themes in one issue.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main outcome
variables.hint: Choose the single most important result this issue should achieve.

help.why: The goal determines emphasis, pacing, CTA strength, and content hierarchy.
help.example: Inform readers
help.avoid: Stacking sell + re-engage + community as equal goals.

variables.label: Target readers
variables.placeholder: Who will receive this newsletter, and what do they care about?
variables.hint: Describe the readers, their context, and their level of familiarity with the topic.

help.why: Audience context materially changes vocabulary, examples, depth, and positioning.
help.example: Who will receive this newsletter, and what do they care about?
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Main takeaway
variables.placeholder: What should readers understand or remember after reading?
variables.hint: State the central idea in one or two clear sentences.

help.why: A defined takeaway prevents the newsletter from becoming unfocused.
help.example: One clear takeaway readers should remember
help.avoid: Vague multi-message issues without a spine.

variables.label: Facts to include
variables.placeholder: List the names, dates, details, prices, results, or points that must be accurate.
variables.hint: Include only information that may safely appear as factual content.

help.why: The newsletter cannot safely infer specific factual details about the user’s subject.
help.example: Only verified dates, prices, and product limits
help.avoid: Invented stats, testimonials, or urgency.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: For example: English, Ukrainian, Polish
variables.hint: Specify the language for all generated newsletter content.

help.why: The language cannot always be inferred from the topic or source material.
help.example: English
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Newsletter format
variables.placeholder: Choose the closest format
variables.hint: This controls the issue’s native editorial structure.

help.why: Different newsletter formats require different section structures and levels of promotion.
help.example: Editorial update
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Sender name
variables.placeholder: Name of the person or team sending the newsletter
variables.hint: Used naturally in the introduction or sign-off when appropriate.

help.why: Sender identity affects voice, perspective, and sign-off wording.
help.example: Name of the person or team sending the newsletter
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Publication or brand
variables.placeholder: Newsletter, company, project, or publication name
variables.hint: Leave blank when the newsletter is sent only under a personal name.

help.why: The publication identity may need to appear in the opening, footer, or sender context.
help.example: Newsletter, company, project, or publication name
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Primary reader action
variables.placeholder: For example: Read the guide, register, reply, or visit the product page
variables.hint: Use Auto to let the model choose one proportional next step.

help.why: The desired action determines the newsletter’s conversion path and closing.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Newsletter length
variables.placeholder: Select the preferred depth
variables.hint: The final length may adjust slightly to fit the supplied information.

help.why: Newsletter depth materially affects structure and the amount of supporting context.
help.example: Standard: 600–900 words
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Source material
variables.placeholder: Paste notes, drafts, research, transcripts, announcements, or source excerpts.
variables.hint: The model should use this material without inventing unsupported additions.

help.why: Raw material helps preserve supplied facts, terminology, and original insights.
help.example: Paste notes, drafts, research, transcripts, announcements, or source excerpts.
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Requested sections
variables.placeholder: For example: introduction, product update, three links, quick tip, closing note
variables.hint: Leave blank for a structure selected automatically from the newsletter format.

help.why: Some publishers have recurring sections that must remain consistent between issues.
help.example: For example: introduction, product update, three links, quick tip, closing note
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Opening approach
variables.placeholder: Choose how the issue should begin
variables.hint: The opening should still remain natural and relevant to the topic.

help.why: The opening approach changes the reader’s entry point without requiring a full writing brief.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Supporting links
variables.placeholder: Add relevant URLs with a short note explaining each destination.
variables.hint: Only supplied links may be included; the model must not invent URLs.

help.why: Curated resources and secondary references require exact user-supplied destinations.
help.example: Add relevant URLs with a short note explaining each destination.
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Dates and timing
variables.placeholder: Add confirmed launch dates, event times, deadlines, availability, or time zones.
variables.hint: Leave blank when the issue is not time-sensitive.

help.why: Time-sensitive details cannot be guessed safely and may affect urgency and placement.
help.example: Add confirmed launch dates, event times, deadlines, availability, or time zones.
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Issue context
variables.placeholder: Mention the issue number, recurring theme, previous issue, or ongoing series.
variables.hint: Use this to maintain continuity with an established newsletter.

help.why: Recurring newsletters may require continuity that cannot be inferred from one issue.
help.example: Mention the issue number, recurring theme, previous issue, or ongoing series.
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Content to avoid
variables.placeholder: List unwanted topics, phrases, comparisons, promises, or editorial angles.
variables.hint: Use for project-specific restrictions rather than general writing rules.

help.why: The user may have brand-specific exclusions that the model cannot predict.
help.example: List unwanted topics, phrases, comparisons, promises, or editorial angles.
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Choose the closest tone
variables.hint: The tone will be adapted to the audience and subject.

help.why: Tone is a meaningful brand preference that cannot always be inferred.
help.example: Clear and natural
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Brand voice notes
variables.placeholder: Describe preferred wording, personality, style, or examples of how the brand speaks.
variables.hint: Do not include general rules such as “write naturally” or “proofread.”

help.why: Specific voice characteristics help the output sound consistent with the sender.
help.example: Describe preferred wording, personality, style, or examples of how the brand speaks.
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Delivery platform
variables.placeholder: Select the publishing service
variables.hint: Used only to adapt formatting and presentation conventions.

help.why: Different newsletter tools support slightly different formatting conventions.
help.example: Platform-neutral
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Formatting style
variables.placeholder: Choose the preferred presentation
variables.hint: The model will avoid layouts that are fragile in email clients.

help.why: Presentation style affects headings, section length, link placement, and visual density.
help.example: Clean email layout
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Personalisation level
variables.placeholder: Choose how personally the newsletter should address readers
variables.hint: No recipient names or personal facts will be invented.

help.why: The preferred relationship between sender and reader changes pronouns and narrative perspective.
help.example: Light reader focus
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Preferred sign-off
variables.placeholder: For example: Thanks for reading, See you next week, or The Creatornivo Team
variables.hint: Use Auto for a natural sign-off based on the sender and tone.

help.why: Established newsletters may use a recognisable closing phrase or signature.
help.example: Auto
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Invite reader replies
variables.placeholder: Not applicable
variables.hint: Adds one natural reply question when reader feedback supports the goal.

help.why: Reply invitations are valuable for some newsletters but inappropriate for others.
help.example: No
help.avoid: Invented subscriber names or private personalisation.

variables.label: Primary CTA URL
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/destination
variables.hint: Leave blank when the CTA is to reply, reflect, or take an offline action.

help.why: The primary conversion destination must be supplied rather than invented.
help.example: https://example.com/destination
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Claims and evidence
variables.placeholder: List approved statistics, results, studies, testimonials, or claims with their support.
variables.hint: Unsupported proof, performance results, and quotations will be omitted.

help.why: Promotional and factual claims require user-supplied evidence and approval.
help.example: List approved statistics, results, studies, testimonials, or claims with their support.
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Commercial disclosures
variables.placeholder: Mention sponsorships, affiliate links, partnerships, gifted products, or paid placements.
variables.hint: The model will add a clear disclosure only when relevant information is supplied.

help.why: Commercial relationships may require transparent disclosure in the issue.
help.example: Mention sponsorships, affiliate links, partnerships, gifted products, or paid placements.
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Sensitive topic category
variables.placeholder: Select only when relevant
variables.hint: Used to apply cautious wording and appropriate verification notes.

help.why: High-stakes subject areas require stronger limitations and verification handling.
help.example: None
help.avoid: Medical/legal claims without approved wording and jurisdiction.

variables.label: Relevant jurisdiction
variables.placeholder: For example: United States, European Union, Ukraine, or California
variables.hint: Specify the applicable country, state, or region when rules may differ.

help.why: Legal, financial, political, employment, and regulated information may depend on location.
help.example: For example: United States, European Union, Ukraine, or California
help.avoid: Invented links, fake personalisation, false urgency, or unsupported claims.
